text: The Certificate in Animation Theory and Practice offered by the Department of Film and Media provides an interdisciplinary opportunity to study animation through hybrid and experimental approaches. The program explores animation history, theory, and production, ranging from Classical Hollywood and Studio Ghibli to experimental and VR/AR animation.

Applied Economics examines how individuals, markets, and governments make decisions related to prices, spending, saving, and policy. The program focuses on understanding material well-being and analyzing real-world economic problems such as environmental pollution and public policy.

The Art History program emphasizes interdisciplinary approaches to the study of visual and material culture. Students explore methodologies including gender studies, critical theory, curatorial studies, and the relationships between art, literature, science, and society across time and cultures.

Artist in Community Education (ACE) is designed for practicing artists interested in education. The program prepares candidates for Ontario College of Teachers Certification and supports teaching at the Primary-Junior or Intermediate-Senior levels.

Astrophysics provides training in theory, computation, statistical modelling, data analysis, and experimental methods. Students develop transferable skills applicable to careers in academia, government research, finance, medicine, technology, and data analytics.

The Biochemistry program explores the chemical processes of living organisms, including cancer progression, cellular communication, infection, inheritance, and disease. Students gain hands-on research experience in molecular genetics, metabolism, bioengineering, and regenerative medicine.

Biology explores life from molecular mechanisms to ecosystems, covering topics such as climate change, biodiversity conservation, evolution, and genomics. The program offers diverse opportunities for studying the natural world across multiple scales.

Biology and Mathematics integrates genetics, genomics, epidemiology, and population ecology to address quantitative challenges in medicine, industry, and research. The program emphasizes interdisciplinary training and quantitative literacy.

Biology and Psychology combines core elements of both disciplines to study brain, behaviour, and cognition. The program emphasizes cross-disciplinary integration and allows for advanced specialization and independent study.

Biomedical Computing combines computer science with life sciences to address health-related challenges. Coursework includes artificial intelligence, computational biology, software development, genetics, and physiology, preparing students for careers in medicine and medical research.

The Biotechnology program is a Subject of Specialization Plan focused on applied research. It balances conceptual and practical learning across biological scales, supporting applications in agriculture, medicine, and environmental sustainability.

Classical Studies examines ancient Greek and Roman civilizations through language, history, mythology, literature, philosophy, art, and archaeology, emphasizing multidisciplinary inquiry and cultural understanding.

Environmental Biology integrates biological sciences with environmental studies to address conservation, pollution, and climate change using genetics, ecology, and physiology.
Environmental Chemistry studies chemical behavior in natural systems and explores how industrial processes can be improved to reduce environmental impact.
Environmental Geology focuses on Earth materials, geological processes, and resource management, with attention to environmental sustainability and extraction impacts.
Environmental Life Sciences examines how environmental factors such as contaminants and water quality affect human health and disease.
Environmental Science integrates scientific analysis with policy and planning to address complex sustainability challenges.
Environmental Studies emphasizes interdisciplinary approaches combining natural sciences, humanities, and social sciences to solve environmental problems.
Environmental Toxicology studies the effects of pollutants on organisms and ecosystems, integrating biology, chemistry, and environmental science.
